perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Definitions In this agreement unless the context otherwise requires: "Agreement" means this agreement; "Confidential Information" means all information which one of the Parties will have access to or come into possession of which is confidential and proprietary to the other Party and which is either declared to be confidential or which the receiving Party should know, acting reasonably, is confidential or proprietary in nature and includes, but is not limited to, the terms and pricing for the Products and Services, all information contained on or accessible through the Partner Portal, as defined hereafter, any information disclosed by any third party which the third party is obligated to treat as confidential or proprietary to one of the Parties hereto, trade secrets, know-how, processes, standards, product specifications, marketing plans and techniques, cost and financial pricing figures, all client or customer information (including without limitation their names, financial information, address or telephone number), all systems software applications, all software/systems source and object code, data, documentation, program files, flow charts, and all operational procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Definitions In this agreement unless the context otherwise requires: "Agreement" means this agreement; "Confidential Information" means all information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing by the disclosing party. This explicitly excludes, but is not limited to, the terms and pricing for the Products and Services, information contained on or accessible through the Partner Portal, trade secrets, and client or customer information, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing; This includes only information related to security protocols.
perturbation.explanation: This change to the definition of 'Confidential Information' introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 4.2 states that 'Galaxy shall treat the Records as Confidential Information,' and the Records include client information. The modified definition explicitly excludes client information from being considered Confidential Information, creating conflict. The original text states that Confidential Information includes a broad range of data, while the modified definition narrows it to specifically designated information related to security protocols.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Ownership and Privacy of Records Galaxy shall own the Records, but it shall use the information contained therein only as allowed by the terms and conditions and intent of this Agreement. Galaxy shall treat the Records as Confidential Information and it shall not provide to or allow access by any third party except as required or allowed by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts Section 4.2. The original definition included client information. The changed definition explicitly excludes it while Section 4.2 mandates that Galaxy treat Records, which includes client information, as Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Approved Products Galaxy shall, at its sole discretion, determine the technical specifications and the brand and model of those devices which it will support in providing the Services and chooses to offer as Products; Telnet shall purchase from Galaxy all devices or Products used or to be used by it to enable the provisioning of the Services pursuant to this Agreement and Galaxy shall be required to support only those devices actually purchased from Galaxy by Telnet; Notwithstanding section 6.1(b), Telnet may request from Galaxy that it enable and support devices other than Products acquired by Telnet from Galaxy, and Galaxy shall not unreasonably refuse such request; and Galaxy shall have the right, at its sole discretion, to change the Products by discontinuing or adding new devices to the Products offered and by changing manufacturers, brands, models or technical or software specifications of any of them.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Approved Products Galaxy shall, at its sole discretion, determine the technical specifications and the brand and model of those devices which it will support in providing the Services and chooses to offer as Products; Telnet is encouraged to purchase from Galaxy all devices or Products used or to be used by it to enable the provisioning of the Services pursuant to this Agreement, however if Telnet has an existing inventory of products, they are allowed to use that. Galaxy shall be required to support all of those devices. Galaxy shall have the right, at its sole discretion, to change the Products by discontinuing or adding new devices to the Products offered and by changing manufacturers, brands, models or technical or software specifications of any of them.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing a mandatory condition ('Telnet shall purchase from Galaxy all devices') with a suggestion. While Galaxy has the right to change which devices are supported in the original and modified text, the modification requires that Galaxy supports all devices Telnet uses, regardless of the original purchase location.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.8 Warranty Unless specifically stated otherwise, Galaxy makes no warranty or guarantee, express or implied, including any implied warranty of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose, with regard to the Products. All warranties with regard to the Products shall be those of the original equipment manufacturer only.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a significant contradiction regarding product warranties. The original text requires Telnet to purchase devices from Galaxy, which implies that Galaxy might offer a warranty (or at least have control over the OEM warranty). The contradicted text (6.8) states that Galaxy makes no warranty for the products, deferring to the original equipment manufacturer. However, the changed text allows Telnet to use existing inventory, but mandates that Galaxy support ALL of those devices. This creates a direct conflict. If Galaxy is required to support devices not purchased from them, yet disclaims all warranties, it becomes unclear how Telnet would obtain support/warranty for non-Galaxy products now supported by Galaxy. The contradiction arises between the obligation to support and the disclaimer of warranty, directly conflicting the meaning of the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10.5 Subscribers If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to section 10.2, Galaxy shall have the right to contact Subscribers directly and solicit such Subscribers to become subscribers of Galaxy, an affiliate thereof or of another client of Galaxy's.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.5 Subscribers If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to section 10.2, Galaxy shall be obligated to contact Subscribers directly and solicit such Subscribers to become subscribers of Galaxy, an affiliate thereof or of another client of Galaxy's. Galaxy will pay Telnet 20% of the net profit from these acquired subscribers for 1 year.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes Galaxy's right to solicit subscribers after termination an obligation and introduces a profit-sharing element. This creates a contradiction with the original agreement, which grants Galaxy the right, but not the obligation, to contact subscribers after termination. Original clause 8.3 states: "Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any damages or compensation in connection with termination of this Agreement including, without limitation, for loss of profits, loss of investment or expenditures made in reliance on this Agreement or loss of goodwill." This inserted modification is contradicting the mentioned text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.3 Liability Upon Termination of Agreement Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any damages or compensation in connection with termination of this Agreement including, without limitation, for loss of profits, loss of investment or expenditures made in reliance on this Agreement or loss of goodwill.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a profit-sharing element after termination, directly contradicting clause 8.3, which explicitly states that neither party is liable for loss of profits or compensation in connection with termination. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the new obligation to pay Telnet 20% of net profit from acquired subscribers for 1 year directly conflicts with the original clause's disclaimer of liability for loss of profits following termination.
